title: Verify lifecycle label progression through pipeline stages
description: The lifecycle label progression feature is already fully implemented in the Kody Engine. Verification confirms: (1) `setLifecycleLabel()` in `dist/bin/cli.js:7061` removes all existing lifecycle labels and sets a new one. (2) `applyPreStageLabel()` in `dist/bin/cli.js:16621` sets labels in sequence: `planning` before plan stage, `building` before build stage, `verifying` before verify stage, `review` before review stage, `fixing` before review-fix stage, `shipping` before ship stage. (3) Line 17873 sets `done` when all pipeline stages complete. (4) Line 17860 sets `failed` on stage failure. The progression planning→building→verifying→review→done matches the implementation path: `kody:planning` → `kody:building` → `kody:verifying` → `kody:review` → `kody:fixing` → `kody:shipping` → `kody:done`. The labels `kody:planning`, `kody:building`, `kody:verifying`, `kody:review`, `kody:done` are all created in the bootstrap code (lines ~5883-5917). The pipeline is currently running (taskify stage active per status.json).
existing_patterns: setLifecycleLabel() function at cli.js:7061 — manages lifecycle labels by removing old ones and setting new one via gh label commands
applyPreStageLabel() at cli.js:16621 — sets per-stage labels before each pipeline stage starts
LIFECYCLE_LABELS constant at cli.js:7425 — defines all valid lifecycle phases
done label set at cli.js:17873 when all STAGES complete
failed label set at cli.js:17860 on pipeline failure
